prompt:
Our CMS keeps mangling article text: one code path escapes ampersands twice ('AT&amp;T' shipped to production), another one un-escapes user comments with a chain of .replace() calls that turns '&lt;' into '<' because it runs the replacements in sequence. I want one audited module, entities.ts, exporting encodeEntities(text, options?) and decodeEntities(text, options?), and everything else deleted.

Encoding. Default mode 'minimal' escapes exactly & < > " ' to & < > " ' and nothing else. Mode 'full' additionally turns characters from a common-names table (at least nbsp, copy, reg, trade, sect, deg, hellip, mdash, ndash, euro, pound) into their named form, and every other character above 0x7E into an uppercase-hex numeric reference like é — one reference per code point, so an emoji is a single 😀, never a surrogate pair. Both modes must be double-encode safe by default: an & that already begins something we could decode (a known name, or a well-formed {/😀) is left alone; an option doubleEncode: true forces the naive behavior. An & that starts an unknown name like &bogus; is NOT protected — encode it.

Decoding. One pass, left to right — the output of decoding must never itself be re-decoded ('&amp;' is '&', full stop). Understands the named table (case-sensitive), decimal references, and hex references with either x or X and any digit case. Two policies via options.mode: 'lenient' (default) leaves anything malformed or unknown exactly as it appeared — bare &, &amp with no semicolon, &bogus;, &#xZZ;. 'strict' throws an Error instead, and the message has to be debuggable: name the offending entity, and for a bare & include its string index. Numeric references that aren't real characters (0, surrogates, above 0x10FFFF) decode to U+FFFD in lenient mode and throw in strict.

test_entities.ts is the contract, `node --test test_entities.ts` runs it, don't touch it. No deps.
